edges.description: PDE8B disruption is causative for a striatal neurodegenerative movement disorder.
Striatal degeneration produces slowly progressive parkinsonism.
Basal ganglia dysfunction manifests as slowed movement.
Basal ganglia dysfunction manifests as rigidity.
Striatal/basal-ganglia disease contributes to dysarthric speech.
Striatal/basal-ganglia disease contributes to impaired gait.
Progressive motor dysfunction can include swallowing impairment.
Motor-circuit dysfunction can impair rapid alternating movements.
Striatal/basal-ganglia involvement can manifest with ataxia in reported ADSD.
ADSN is classified clinically as a movement disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant striatal neurodegeneration
evidence.snippet: HP:0100022 | Abnormality of movement | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Ataxia is reported in a PDE8B-confirmed ADSN family.

evidence.snippet: For the first time, we described the typical ataxia phenotype in ADSD.

nodes.description: ADSN-associated heterozygous PDE8B frameshift or nonsense variants disrupt cyclic nucleotide phosphodiesterase function. PDE8B normally degrades cyclic AMP, a second messenger involved in dopamine signaling and movement control.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration is caused by a mutation in the phosphodiesterase 8B gene.
evidence.snippet: Here we show that ADSD is caused by a complex frameshift mutation (c.94G>C+c.95delT) in the phosphodiesterase 8B (PDE8B) gene, which results in a loss of enzymatic phosphodiesterase activity.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration is caused by a mutation in the phosphodiesterase 8B gene.
evidence.snippet: PDE8B degrades cyclic AMP, a second messenger implied in dopamine signaling.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation of PDE8B Gene in a Japanese family with autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: We found a heterozygous nonsense mutation in the first exon of cyclic nucleotide phosphodiesterase 8B gene, which is predicted to disrupt all important functional domains of the cyclic nucleotide phosphodiesterase 8B protein.

meta.association: Heterozygous germline PDE8B pathogenic variants cause ADSN.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation of PDE8B Gene in a Japanese family with autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSIONS: This family is the second family with autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration after the first German family, confirming that cyclic nucleotide phosphodiesterase 8B gene is the causative gene for this disease.

nodes.description: Slowly progressive parkinsonism is a reported core clinical presentation.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation of PDE8B Gene in a Japanese family with autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration is a rare autosomal-dominant neurodegenerative movement disorder characterized by slowly progressive parkinsonism.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical findings of autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration and PDE8B mutation screening in parkinsonism and related disorders.
evidence.snippet: Clinically, the patient exhibited progressive parkinsonism without tremor and ataxia phenotype.

nodes.description: ADSN primarily affects the basal ganglia/striatal system, with MRI lesions restricted mainly to the putamen and caudate nucleus or high T2 signal in the striatum.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration is caused by a mutation in the phosphodiesterase 8B gene.
evidence.snippet: PDE8B is highly expressed in the brain, especially in the putamen, which is affected by ADSD.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant striatal degeneration (ADSD): clinical description and mapping to 5q13-5q14.
evidence.snippet: MRI demonstrated a distinctive lesion pattern restricted mainly to the putamen and caudate nucleus.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation of PDE8B Gene in a Japanese family with autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: Clinical features of the patients are slowly progressive parkinsonism and brain MRI showing high signal intensity in T2-weighted images in the striatum.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical findings of autosomal-dominant striatal degeneration and PDE8B mutation screening in parkinsonism and related disorders.
evidence.snippet: Neuroimaging showed an inhomogeneous increased signal in the patient's striatum on T1-weighted images but a decreased signal in the presymptomatic carrier.
